rationale: The cited paper is used in the methods section as concrete precedent for key design and estimation choices in Study 1, especially using a ratings-based conjoint approach and a random-intercept specification. That is a functional role in how the empirical test is set up, not just a broad literature-list mention. At the same time, the citing paper does not build on the cited paper's triadic channel theory; it uses it mainly as methodological support.
relation_description: The citing paper applies methodological elements associated with Wuyts et al. (2004) to its own review-platform trust experiments, particularly conjoint design and model specification choices. The relation is methodological transfer to a new substantive context.
